content: Multi-touch pitfalls: (1) touch-action:none must be set on interactive areas or browser consumes touchmove for scroll. (2) iOS Safari makes touchstart/touchmove passive by default since iOS 11.3, preventDefault() is ignored unless {passive:false} is set. (3) Touch identifiers are reused and non-sequential - must track by touch.identifier not array index. (4) changedTouches vs touches vs targetTouches confusion causes missed touchend events. (5) touchcancel/pointercancel not handled causes stuck game state when incoming call or system dialog interrupts. (6) Double-tap zoom on iOS interferes with tap gestures - need touch-action:manipulation. (7) Overscroll bounce on iOS needs overscroll-behavior:none on html/body. (8) Element removal during touch sequence breaks subsequent events.
